pr.title: Phase 1 complete: capability tests, dual-DB verification, generic DB server
pr.body:
## Summary

- **Capability test battery** — 64 unit tests pass (up from 37). Added `tests/test_prompt_battery.py` (19 in-process scenarios: all realistic Claude usage patterns, DML rejection, routing correctness for both DBs) and `tests/integration/test_mcp_capabilities.py` (28 live-server tests covering all 8 requirement areas).
- **Dual-DB verification** — confirmed both `saaz_demo` and `neon_purple_kite` are reachable via MCP. The initial `neon_list_tables` SSL error was a cold-start stale pool connection, not a config problem. neon-purple-kite DB is connected (PostgreSQL 17.10) with an empty public schema; `neon_auth` schema has 9 Neon-managed auth tables.
- **Generic DB server cleanup** — deleted `neon_mcp/server.py` shim entirely. `db_mcp/server.py` is now the one and only DB server binary. Adding a third database requires zero Python — just env vars and a `.replit` workflow task.
- **Replit agent prompt** — `docs/REPLIT_AGENT_NEW_DB.md` — a self-contained prompt to paste into Replit Agent to provision, seed, wire, and verify any new database against mneme end-to-end.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `tests/test_prompt_battery.py` | New: 19 in-process Claude usage scenarios |
| `tests/integration/test_mcp_capabilities.py` | New: 28 live-server capability tests |
| `neon_mcp/server.py` + `neon_mcp/__init__.py` | **Deleted** — was a 5-line shim, now unnecessary |
| `.replit` | `Start neon-mcp` workflow now runs `db_mcp.server:app` with inline env vars |
| `db_mcp/server.py` | Updated docstring: "Adding a new database (no new files needed)" recipe |
| `docs/STATUS.md` | Probe results table, corrected neon findings, updated test counts |
| `docs/REPLIT_AGENT_NEW_DB.md` | New: Replit Agent prompt for adding any new database |

## Architecture: adding a new database (no Python needed)

```toml
# .replit — add one task per new DB
[[workflows.workflow.tasks]]
task = "shell.exec"
args = "DB_MCP_TOOL_PREFIX=acme DB_MCP_NAME=acme-prod DB_MCP_DATABASE_URL_ENV=DATABASE_URL_ACME uvicorn db_mcp.server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3001"
waitForPort = 3001
```

Then register in `UPSTREAM_DB_MCP_SERVERS` and `NAMESPACE_ROUTING_KEYWORDS`. Done.

## Replit Agent prompt usage

Open `docs/REPLIT_AGENT_NEW_DB.md`, replace the `<PLACEHOLDER>` values for your dataset, and paste the inner prompt block into Replit Agent. It walks the agent through all 6 steps:
1. Provision DB via Replit tooling
2. Create schema + seed rows
3. Add `.replit` workflow task (no Python)
4. Register namespace in env vars
5. Verify healthz / tools/list / smoke query / audit / DML rejection
6. Persist entry via `register_database` tool

## Test plan

- [x] `make test` — 64 pass, 18 skip (no DATABASE_URL in CI)
- [x] Live MCP probe — all saaz tools respond correctly
- [x] Live MCP probe — all neon tools respond correctly (empty public schema)
- [x] DML rejected on both DBs (`saaz_query`, `neon_query`)
- [x] Semantic search ranked correctly (pgvector, 100% embedding coverage)
- [x] `neon_mcp` package fully removed, no dangling imports
- [x] `.replit` workflow boots `db_mcp.server:app` with env vars (no shim)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01V3UHmTqWKdktLf76J34dAj
pr.author: aliomraniH
